findings.description: No h1 element exists in the HTML. The document title/header information is placed in a div with class 'page-header' as plain paragraph text rather than as a proper heading. The two section headings ('Background' and 'Public Comment & Public Hearing') are rendered as h2 elements, which is reasonable, but without an h1 the heading hierarchy is incomplete.
findings.evidence:
The PDF header 'FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale...' serves as the document title but is rendered as

...

instead of an

.

findings.description: The URL to the Administrative Record is present in the HTML but is not rendered as a clickable hyperlink. The PDF shows it as a hyperlink (https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx) but the HTML renders it as plain text.
findings.evidence:
PDF: 'https://edocs.deq.nc.gov/WasteManagement/Welcome.aspx' appears as a hyperlink. HTML: rendered as plain text within a

tag without an element.

findings.description: The page header block (FACT SHEET / DRAFT PERMIT RENEWAL / Safety-Kleen Archdale / address / EPA ID) is repeated three times in the HTML — once in the outer page-header div, once between pages, and once for page 2 header. While the PDF does repeat headers on each page, the HTML has an additional outer wrapper that adds a third instance.
findings.evidence: The header block appears in: (1) first page-header div, (2) second page-header div, plus the overall document has no consolidation.

findings.description: The HTML uses multiple nested <main> elements (an outer <main> from the body wrapper, plus two inner <main> elements for page 1 and page 2 content), which is invalid HTML structure and misrepresents the document structure.
findings.evidence: The outer <body> contains <main>, which itself contains additional <main> elements for page 1 and page 2 content sections.
